attributes.magic: PE32 executable for MS Windows (GUI) Intel 80386 32-bit

trid.file_type: Excel Microsoft Office Open XML Format document

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious New Instance Of An Office COM Object
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects an svchost process spawning an instance of an office application. This happens when the initial word application creates an instance of one of the Office COM objects such as 'Word.Application', 'Excel.Application', etc.
This can be used by malicious actors to create malicious Office documents with macros on the fly. (See vba2clr project in the references)

trid.file_type: Excel Microsoft Office Open XML Format document
Word Microsoft Office Open XML Format document

attributes.magic: New Line Delimited JSON text data

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis:
The analysis indicates the PDF file is benign. A technical examination of its internal structure found no evidence of a malicious execution chain, such as automatic actions, embedded JavaScript, or other executable code. The visual analysis corroborates this, identifying the document as a professionally presented research report on a national security topic with no signs of social engineering or phishing.

The primary technical finding of note is a significant structural anomaly, with 93 of 95 internal streams failing to decode. While such behavior can be used for obfuscation, it is considered a non-threatening structural error in this case due to the complete absence of an associated trigger or malicious payload. The document contains numerous URIs, but they are configured as standard, user-initiated hyperlinks.

Ultimately, the lack of any technical mechanism for malicious execution, combined with a visual layer that appears to be a legitimate and static informational document, supports the conclusion that the file does not pose a threat.

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature identifies Adobe Extensible Metadata Platform (XMP) identifiers embedded within files. Defined as a standard for mapping graphical asset relationships, XMP allows for tracking of both parent-child relationships and individual revisions. There are three categories of identifiers: original document, document, and instance. Generally, XMP data is stored in XML format, updated on save/copy, and embedded within the graphical asset. These identifiers can be used to track both malicious and benign graphics within common Microsoft and Adobe document lures.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature detects an Adobe Type 1 Font. The Type 1 Font Format is a standardized font format for digital imaging applications.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: ET INFO Observed Google DNS over HTTPS Domain (dns .google in TLS SNI)

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: ET INFO Observed Google DNS over HTTPS Domain (dns .google in TLS SNI)
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert tls $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"ET INFO Observed Google DNS over HTTPS Domain (dns .google in TLS SNI)"; flow:established,to_server; threshold: type both, track by_src, count 1, seconds 600; tls.sni; dotprefix; content:".dns.google"; endswith; reference:url,developers.google.com/speed/public-dns/docs/doh/; classtype:misc-activity; sid:2047866; rev:4; metadata:affected_product Any, attack_target Client_Endpoint, created_at 2022_02_07, deployment Perimeter, performance_impact Low, confidence High, signature_severity Informational, tag DoH, updated_at 2023_10_05, reviewed_at 2023_10_05, former_sid 2851058; target:src_ip;)

crowdsourced_yara_results.author: JPCERT/CC Incident Response Group

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature detects the presence of a number of Windows API functionality often seen within embedded executables. When this signature alerts on an executable, it is not an indication of malicious behavior. However, if seen firing in other file types, deeper investigation may be warranted.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

There are three categories of identifiers: original document, document, and instance. Generally, XMP data is stored in XML format, updated on save/copy, and embedded within the graphical asset. These identifiers can be used to track both malicious and benign graphics within common Microsoft and Adobe document lures.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature detects an Adobe Type 1 Font. The Type 1 Font Format is a standardized font format for digital imaging applications.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: ET INFO Observed Google DNS over HTTPS Domain (dns .google in TLS SNI)
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert tls $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"ET INFO Observed Google DNS over HTTPS Domain (dns .google in TLS SNI)"; flow:established,to_server; threshold: type both, track by_src, count 1, seconds 600; tls.sni; dotprefix; content:".dns.google"; endswith; reference:url,developers.google.com/speed/public-dns/docs/doh/; classtype:misc-activity; sid:2047866; rev:4; metadata:affected_product Any, attack_target Client_Endpoint, created_at 2022_02_07, deployment Perimeter, performance_impact Low, confidence High, signature_severity Informational, tag DoH, updated_at 2023_10_05, reviewed_at 2023_10_05, former_sid 2851058; target:src_ip;)
